extends: CLAUDE.md banned-phrase reference + scripts/copy_voice_constants.ts (authoritative). This file adds lead-specific decisions only.
register: plain, grounded, confident-without-boasting British English; tradesperson talking straight to a homeowner. Short sentences. Concrete nouns (floors, grout, set-out, levelling), not adjectives.

naming.decision: Use Kyle's first name freely in copy. Basis: the business is 'Kyle Knowles Tiling' and Kyle is named in 4 of 5 reviews. This deliberately overrides brief.json contact_name_usage_allowed=false (that flag guards against using an UNKNOWN name lifted from review text; Kyle is the named proprietor in the business name itself).
naming.third_vs_first_person: Default to a light third person ('Kyle levels the floors...'). A first-person line is allowed only if strictly factual (no invented backstory, no specific years). Do not write an owner-monologue: there is no self-description from Kyle in evidence.

the_angle.name: the fitter others call when it's too complex
the_angle.how_to_use: Lead with it, state it as fact, never as a put-down of named competitors. Frame: Kyle takes the difficult and the abandoned jobs. Anchored to reviews[1] (Luca, previous tiler went AWOL) and reviews[2] (Stephanie, 'too complex' for the other fitter). Acceptable phrasings: 'the job another fitter turned down', 'picked up where a previous tiler walked off', 'the patterns others call too complex'. Do not invent the competitor or the outcome beyond what the two reviews state.

proof_rules.google: State exactly: '4.9 on Google' and '43 Google reviews' (both sourced=true in brief.json). '40+ reviews' is an acceptable evergreen variant. Attribute the rating to Google.
proof_rules.no_other_proof: Do NOT cite Yell / MyBuilder / Trustpilot - those probes are low-confidence, exact-name-only, unverified (brief.json directory_probes). Do NOT claim any certification, membership, insurance, or 'qualified/certified' - none verified.
proof_rules.experience: Laura's review says 'many years of experience'. You may say 'years of experience' softly as a customer observation, but never a specific number of years and never as a first-person claim.

review_attribution: First name + 'Google review' (e.g. 'Stephanie, Google review'). No surnames (none known). Never use a reviewer's first name as a section headline (the 'Steve' anti-pattern).
banned_for_this_business: any phrase on the CLAUDE.md / copy_voice_constants.ts lists (generic, provenance, meta-provenance, stat sub-labels)
'tiling sorted', 'tiling done properly', 'tiling done right' as a hero/standalone slogan (generic-trade filler)
claiming areas beyond Manchester / Openshaw / East Manchester / M11
the street address (51 Lees St) anywhere public
first-person claims of qualifications, insurance, or years trading
meta lines like 'customers mention' / 'reviews describe' - state the thing directly instead
allowed_words_from_reviews_in_quotes_onl: Words like 'professional', 'expert', 'amazing', '10/10' appear in real review text. They are fine INSIDE attributed verbatim quotes, but must NOT appear in Kyle's own/site prose (CLAUDE.md salesy ban).
punctuation: No em/en dashes anywhere. Use ' - ', comma, or full stop. British spelling (levelling, colour, tiled).
cta_language: Quote-only sticky and primary CTA: 'Get a quote'. Secondary: 'Call Kyle' / the number. Never put the phone in the sticky CTA (sticky_cta check).
